Title: I love the Act + Acre brand, great new addition!!
ReviewText: Gifted by Act + Acre. The Act + Acre Hydro Serum is a brand new addition to my routine, especially with how dry my hair and scalp have been after years of coloring. It’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave any greasy residue. The multi-molecular hyaluronic acid definitely helps with hydration—I notice less flaking and irritation when I use it consistently. The amino acids and glycerin also seem to help with scalp health, though I can’t say I’ve seen a major difference in hair strength just yet. I apply it to my damp scalp after washing, massage it in, and then put my hair in a loose braid. When I take it out, I get soft, natural waves without feeling like there’s any product buildup. The fact that it’s clean, vegan, and free of harsh ingredients is a plus. While it’s not an instant miracle fix, it does make my scalp feel healthier and my hair look more hydrated over time. If you struggle with dryness or irritation, it’s worth trying—but it works best when you use it consistently.

Title: A must read review. A MATURE skin must have
ReviewText:
I have used this product for years and purchase it exclusively from reputable Korean retailers. As a 51-year-old skincare enthusiast who performs biweekly chemical peels, my skin is extremely reactive and sensitive — yet this line has never caused irritation or breakouts for me.

I believe part of the misunderstanding around this brand comes from how heavily we rely on active ingredients in the U.S. While actives can be beneficial, overuse often leads to dehydration, dullness, and a compromised barrier. What makes this line exceptional is its focus on restoring the skin’s foundational lipids — particularly cholesterol and fatty acids — which are essential for rebuilding the barrier, improving texture, softening the look of pores, and restoring plumpness.

The light blue version is ideal for daytime use due to its lighter texture, while the white version provides deeper overnight support. I consistently wake up with calm, balanced, resilient skin.

This brand is widely respected in Korea for its use of MLE (Multi-Lamellar Emulsion) technology, which mimics the skin’s natural lipid structure and helps reinforce barrier integrity at a structural level. That formulation approach is what truly sets it apart.

In my experience, few products available in the U.S. market compare in terms of barrier support and long-term skin health.

Title: Formula Changed, Less Occlusive
ReviewText:

In plain English, it doesn’t lock in moisture and hydration as much anymore.

Title: I think they changed the formula.
ReviewText:

The sample I received was very rich, and I could see and feel a noticeable difference in my skin after using it for about two weeks. I could tell it was actually repairing my skin barrier because it cleared up my acne and redness. 
Unfortunately, when I used the full sized product, I could immediately tell it wasn't the same. It was of a thinner consistency, it didn't make my skin feel as moisturized, and I didn't get the same results I did with the sample size.  😢

Title: I really wanted to like this...
ReviewText:
Disclaimers: 
- I never used the old version of this, and I only used this product twice. 
- My skin is very sensitive/reactive. 
- My normal moisturizer is Cetaphil moisturizing cream, and my comparisons use this as a control.

I heard good things about this moisturizer, and wanted to try something different, and of course I have been wanting to steer more towards Korean skincare as its gentler and often times more affordable. However, after two uses:
- I noticed that this cream is not as moisturizing as my Cetaphil cream, I could feel some tightness in my skin, even after applying another layer. 
- It didn't make me feel greasy or look oily, however, it did feel like it was getting into my eyes and making them feel filmy, if you know what I mean. 
- It didn't irritate my skin or make it sore/itchy, however I did get a few small whiteheads on my cheeks and forehead, and also developed a pimple under my nostril. I'm not super acne prone, so this was a red flag for me. 
- And for those who are sensitive to smells, it is unscented, but it does have that unscented scent which was also a little off for me.

Is it horrible? No. It will work well for many people. Did it work for me? Unfortunately, no.

ReviewText: At the beginning, I kind of liked this product especially because it spreads so well. However, the more I used it, the more I realized of two things: 1) my skin started to feel more dehydrated than usual (I have oily dehydrated skin) and my eyes would get so irritated in the evening and in the morning they would burn. No cream has ever caused me this no matter how close I get to the eye area. This cream is greasy but I didn’t care because I used it at night, however, it wasn’t effective at all and because it is not hydrating, my barrier feels worse. It’s great for the hands though.

Title: Great Texture, Terrible Absorption
ReviewText: I’ve been using this for almost two months, and I wish I was more impressed. The texture is beautiful, but it takes so long to absorb into my skin. There’s no stickiness, but just feeling it sit on my face for what feels like 17 light years is very annoying. It’s a guessing game each time trying not to apply way too much or way too little. Also, I haven’t found it to be moisturizing. If anything, the texture of my skin has gotten worse. The cold weather where I live could be a contributing factor, but I’m still disappointed that such a nice feeling cream left my skin congested and dehydrated (and sometimes a little flaky). If you’re sensitive or acne-prone, I’d either give this a patch test or skip it unfortunately.

Title: Great night time moisturizer!
ReviewText:
This moisturizer is deeply hydrating. My skin feels so plump and comfortable when I use it, especially at night. It gives that rich, cocooned feeling without actually clogging my pores or breaking me out.

The texture is interesting — it feels oily going on, but it’s not greasy in a heavy or pore-clogging way. It kind of melts into the skin and creates a protective barrier, which I love for nighttime.

For my dry girlies: I am super, super dry, and this is incredibly hydrating at night. I wish I could use it in the morning too because my skin drinks it up. But alas… I cannot. The slight oily slip makes it tricky under makeup and doesn’t sit the best under foundation, so it’s not my favorite for daytime wear.

Overall, it’s an amazing nighttime moisturizer for dry or barrier-compromised skin. Just not my first choice for daytime under makeup. 4/5!

ReviewText: Atobarrier 365 Cream with Ceramide is a standout product for anyone seeking deep hydration and skin barrier repair. Perfect for dry, sensitive, or even compromised skin, it provides an immediate soothing effect while helping to restore the skin’s lipid barrier over time. After a few days of consistent use, you’ll notice your skin feels more supple and resilient, with a noticeable reduction in dryness or irritation. It’s especially beneficial for people with conditions like eczema or those living in harsh climates where the skin is constantly exposed to environmental stressors. The fragrance-free formula is a bonus for sensitive skin, and it’s suitable for all skin types, even those prone to allergies. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and nourishes skin • Contains ceramides to restore the skin barrier • Lightweight, fast-absorbing formula • Fragrance-free, suitable for sensitive skin • Great for dry or irritated skin, including eczema Cons: • May not be rich enough for very dry skin types in extreme conditions • On the pricier side for a drugstore moisturizer Overall, Atobarrier 365 is a reliable, gentle cream that lives up to its claims of providing long-lasting moisture and skin barrier repair. It’s a solid choice for daily use, especially if you need something that works without causing irritation.

Title: Amazing to have
ReviewText: Atobarrier 365 Hydro Cera-HA Serum is a game-changer for anyone looking to deeply hydrate and strengthen their skin. This serum blends the power of ceramides and hyaluronic acid, two key ingredients that work in harmony to boost hydration and repair the skin’s protective barrier. Ceramides help restore the skin’s natural barrier function, while hyaluronic acid draws moisture deep into the skin for long-lasting hydration. The texture of the serum is lightweight and slightly gel-like, making it perfect for layering under other products. It absorbs quickly without leaving any stickiness or residue, which makes it ideal for both morning and nighttime routines. After consistent use, you’ll notice that your skin feels smoother, plumper, and more resilient. It also does a great job at calming dry or irritated areas, making it a good option for sensitive skin. One of the standout features is its ability to provide intense hydration without feeling heavy or greasy. If you live in a dry climate or struggle with dehydrated skin, this serum can be a game-changer. Plus, the formula is free from fragrances and irritating additives, which is great for those with sensitive or reactive skin. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and plumps skin with hyaluronic acid • Restores skin barrier with ceramides • Lightweight, non-greasy formula that absorbs quickly • Calms and soothes sensitive or dry skin • Fragrance-free, ideal for sensitive skin Cons: • May need additional moisturizing for very dry skin in extreme conditions • Slightly higher price point than other serums in the same category Overall, the Atobarrier 365 Hydro Cera-HA Serum is an excellent addition to any skincare routine, especially for those seeking deep hydration and barrier repair. Its gentle yet effective formula is perfect for those with sensitive, dry, or irritated skin. It delivers visible results in terms of smoother, more hydrated skin without overwhelming the complexion.

Title: A must read review. A MATURE skin must have
ReviewText:
I have used this product for years and purchase it exclusively from reputable Korean retailers. As a 51-year-old skincare enthusiast who performs biweekly chemical peels, my skin is extremely reactive and sensitive — yet this line has never caused irritation or breakouts for me.

I believe part of the misunderstanding around this brand comes from how heavily we rely on active ingredients in the U.S. While actives can be beneficial, overuse often leads to dehydration, dullness, and a compromised barrier. What makes this line exceptional is its focus on restoring the skin’s foundational lipids — particularly cholesterol and fatty acids — which are essential for rebuilding the barrier, improving texture, softening the look of pores, and restoring plumpness.

The light blue version is ideal for daytime use due to its lighter texture, while the white version provides deeper overnight support. I consistently wake up with calm, balanced, resilient skin.

This brand is widely respected in Korea for its use of MLE (Multi-Lamellar Emulsion) technology, which mimics the skin’s natural lipid structure and helps reinforce barrier integrity at a structural level. That formulation approach is what truly sets it apart.

In my experience, few products available in the U.S. market compare in terms of barrier support and long-term skin health.

ReviewText: This is the most amazing moisturizer for dry, sensitive, and acne prone skin. It layers beautifully under makeup and doesn’t make my face products pill like others do. This moisturizer is the perfect winter weather cream!

Title: Amazing

Title: Gentle yet effective moisturizer
ReviewText: This Aestura AtoBarrier Cream has been a life saver this Winter! Normally I get dry patches on my nostrils and cheeks but since using this I have zero issues! This cream is rich and moisturizing. There is no scent and does not irritate my sensitive skin. Very gentle but effective.

Title: Perfect with tretinoin
ReviewText: I have dry and dehydrated skin. I started tretinoin a while ago. At the time, I was using the Avene TriXera Nutrition balm until it got discontinued. I wanted to try the Aestura cream because it has ceramides and cholesterol similar to the Avene. My skin was peeling and burning as expected from tretinoin. When I used the Aestura cream, it calmed down the burning sensation and smoothed the peeling skin. A year later, my skin has adapted to tretinoin without any issues. I use tretinoin 3 times a week and I use the Aestura cream every night. I wake up with moisturized skin in the morning. I don't use this product in the morning because it's a bit heavy under my sunscreen. I use the Cosrx hyaluronic acid cream instead because it's super lightweight. The only issue I have with this product is that the spreadability is not that great. Because I have dry skin, I apply moisturizers when my face is still a little bit wet or damp, and I find that this product doesn't spread nicely on top of wet skin. Other than that, it's a fantastic product and I will continue to buy it. One bottle lasted me 5 months! It's pricey, but for me it's worth it.

I love korean beauty and im glad it's finally making it's way here to the US. This cream is exactly what i expected it to be and more. I live in a colder state where it's been negatives recently and my skin has been so dry. With this cream my skin has felt so hydrated and nourished. My skin loved it so much and it did not break me out. It also worked well with my other skincare and makeup

Title: visible ceramides
ReviewText: The ATOBARRIER365 Cream with Ceramides is hydrating and I love the visible ceramides. I was curious about the differences between this brand's products as they are all labelled for hydration/moisturization and the skin barrier. They all hydrate my dry skin. I believe the soothing cream is best for the skin barrier. The lotion has a similar texture when applying but maybe slightly lighter. The regular cream contains visible ceramide capsules that burst open when applied to the skin. I feel that only one or two of these products are necessary. I would apply the cream first, followed by the soothing cream, as it is thicker and helps to lock in the ceramides.

Title: Effective
ReviewText: Aestura atobarrier cream is an interesting product. The packaging is very minimalistic but functional. The product itself comes out as a thick cream. Once you start to press it into the skin, it just melts into the skin effortlessly. I look forward to seeing long term effects from this product for those times where my barrier feels compromised by too many actives.

Title: Love love love!!
ReviewText: I’ve been using this product for years!! I’m so in love and so happy for it to come to Sephora!! It’s a staple for acne prone and dry skin girls!❤️

I have had the driest skin this season and couldn’t wait to give this product a try! I love how you can see the capsules of ceramides in the barrier cream. They blend seamlessly into the skin and leave my skin glowy and hydrated!

Title: So good??!!
ReviewText: I have eczema and super dry skin. This is honestly one of my top 3 moisturizers. It is so good at sealing in the moisture into my skin. I notice that it pills ever so slightly however it is so worth it.

I also found it to be good value for money, as you only need a small amount of the product for your entire face and neck.

Title: The gold standard in barrier repair!
ReviewText:
Got a damaged skin barrier from tret or over-exfoliation? Wind burn? Sad sensitive skin in general? My friend, this is THE CREAM FOR YOU. 

This has been my holy grail barrier repair cream for years. Seriously desert-island, “if I could only own a single skincare product for the rest of my life” stuff. I’ve been purchasing it on Korean import sites, and I’m so excited that more people are getting easy access to it now. I plan to switch to Sephora when I run out of my current tube since the ingredients are identical.

I’ve got the sort of face that gets irritated if you look at it wrong: rosacea, hormonal acne, extreme fragrance sensitivities, apparent inability to hold onto moisture, the works. Nothing has been better for me than this at night and in the winter, with the corresponding Hydro cream (blue tube, a little lighter in texture) in the morning and during humid summer weather. 

If you’re like me, you’ll understand when I say this stuff is boring in the best way. It’s absolutely packed with soothing and moisturizing ingredients that have years of science behind them. It applies easily and absorbs surprisingly quickly for something so dense. It’s amazing. I love it. I hope you’ll love it too.

Title: Love
ReviewText: I absolutely love this cream. It’s my go to whenever I need an extra layer of protection and moisture. I highly recommend for all that get facial treatments during winter as this has helped my skin from purging.  It leaves my skin glowing!

Title: Dry skin savior

Title: Pairs well with tretinoin

Title: no longer acne safe!!
ReviewText: i’m absolutely heartbroken that this formula changed and now contains pore-clogging ingredients. this moisturizer was my absolute favorite and i had no idea how i lived without it for so long…. now i will not be repurchasing again.

Title: Saved my dry and flakey forehead!
ReviewText: Saw the reviews and had this on my list as a must-try. Super happy I did along with their other products! Huge improvement to dryness and flakiness on my forehead and cheeks after 1 week of use. The mist product has also been a great add on for daily on-the-go hydration. HAPPY!

Title: Formula Changed, Less Occlusive
ReviewText:
I used to buy this abroad or shipped from Korea. The formula change of removing high grade silicones and waxes in exchange for Niacinamide just turns this to another ceramide moisturizer. Might as well buy CeraVa, Vanicream or Prequel you know? The little balls are just gimmicky now. Plus now it’s less occlusive. In plain English, it doesn’t lock in moisture and hydration as much anymore. 

And Niacinamide is an excellent ingredient for brightening (any dermatologist would agree), but it’s a no-no for the usual target market (former I guess?) of this product who have reallly realllly sensitive, irritated skin who wants BARRIER repair with no actives. Some might still be okay with the Niacinamide, but think post tretinoin, eczema or rosacea.

Title: Amazing - esp when skin barrier has been damaged

Title: I really wanted to like this...
ReviewText:
Disclaimers: 
- I never used the old version of this, and I only used this product twice. 
- My skin is very sensitive/reactive. 
- My normal moisturizer is Cetaphil moisturizing cream, and my comparisons use this as a control.

I heard good things about this moisturizer, and wanted to try something different, and of course I have been wanting to steer more towards Korean skincare as its gentler and often times more affordable. However, after two uses:
- I noticed that this cream is not as moisturizing as my Cetaphil cream, I could feel some tightness in my skin, even after applying another layer. 
- It didn't make me feel greasy or look oily, however, it did feel like it was getting into my eyes and making them feel filmy, if you know what I mean. 
- It didn't irritate my skin or make it sore/itchy, however I did get a few small whiteheads on my cheeks and forehead, and also developed a pimple under my nostril. I'm not super acne prone, so this was a red flag for me. 
- And for those who are sensitive to smells, it is unscented, but it does have that unscented scent which was also a little off for me.

Is it horrible? No. It will work well for many people. Did it work for me? Unfortunately, no.

Title: Great Texture, Terrible Absorption
ReviewText: I’ve been using this for almost two months, and I wish I was more impressed. The texture is beautiful, but it takes so long to absorb into my skin. There’s no stickiness, but just feeling it sit on my face for what feels like 17 light years is very annoying. It’s a guessing game each time trying not to apply way too much or way too little. Also, I haven’t found it to be moisturizing. If anything, the texture of my skin has gotten worse. The cold weather where I live could be a contributing factor, but I’m still disappointed that such a nice feeling cream left my skin congested and dehydrated (and sometimes a little flaky). If you’re sensitive or acne-prone, I’d either give this a patch test or skip it unfortunately.

Title: Great night time moisturizer!
ReviewText:
This moisturizer is deeply hydrating. My skin feels so plump and comfortable when I use it, especially at night. It gives that rich, cocooned feeling without actually clogging my pores or breaking me out.

The texture is interesting — it feels oily going on, but it’s not greasy in a heavy or pore-clogging way. It kind of melts into the skin and creates a protective barrier, which I love for nighttime.

For my dry girlies: I am super, super dry, and this is incredibly hydrating at night. I wish I could use it in the morning too because my skin drinks it up. But alas… I cannot. The slight oily slip makes it tricky under makeup and doesn’t sit the best under foundation, so it’s not my favorite for daytime wear.

Overall, it’s an amazing nighttime moisturizer for dry or barrier-compromised skin. Just not my first choice for daytime under makeup. 4/5!

Title: A new must-have

Title: You need to have this
ReviewText: Atobarrier 365 Cream with Ceramide is a standout product for anyone seeking deep hydration and skin barrier repair. This rich, yet lightweight moisturizer is formulated with ceramides, which are key for strengthening the skin’s natural barrier and locking in moisture. Perfect for dry, sensitive, or even compromised skin, it provides an immediate soothing effect while helping to restore the skin’s lipid barrier over time. The texture is silky, absorbing quickly without leaving any greasy residue, which makes it a great choice for both daytime and nighttime use. After a few days of consistent use, you’ll notice your skin feels more supple and resilient, with a noticeable reduction in dryness or irritation. It’s especially beneficial for people with conditions like eczema or those living in harsh climates where the skin is constantly exposed to environmental stressors. The fragrance-free formula is a bonus for sensitive skin, and it’s suitable for all skin types, even those prone to allergies. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and nourishes skin • Contains ceramides to restore the skin barrier • Lightweight, fast-absorbing formula • Fragrance-free, suitable for sensitive skin • Great for dry or irritated skin, including eczema Cons: • May not be rich enough for very dry skin types in extreme conditions • On the pricier side for a drugstore moisturizer Overall, Atobarrier 365 is a reliable, gentle cream that lives up to its claims of providing long-lasting moisture and skin barrier repair. It’s a solid choice for daily use, especially if you need something that works without causing irritation.
